title: tephroseris lanceolata
intro: Tephroseris lanceolata, also known as the alpine marigold or lance-leaved hawk's beard, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing. Tephroseris lanceolata can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
